meta.name: CBI Copenhagen Burnout Inventory
meta.description: The CBI is a 19-item scale assessing burnout across three domains: personal, work-related, and client-related burnout.
meta.author: Kristensen TS, et al. (2005)
meta.reference: Kristensen TS, et al. Work & Stress. 2005;19(3):192-207.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how often each statement applies to you based on your experience over the past few weeks.

questions.text: How often do you feel tired?
How often are you physically exhausted?
How often are you emotionally exhausted?
How often do you think: "I can't take it anymore"?
How often do you feel worn out?
How often do you feel weak and susceptible to illness?
Do you feel burnt out because of your work?
Do you feel worn out at the end of the working day?
Are you exhausted in the morning at the thought of another day at work?
Do you feel that every working hour is tiring for you?
Do you have enough energy for family and friends during leisure time?
Do you find it hard to work with clients/patients?
Do you find it frustrating to work with clients/patients?
Does it drain your energy to work with clients/patients?
Do you feel that you give more than you get back when you work with clients/patients?
Are you tired of working with clients/patients?
Do you sometimes wonder how long you will be able to continue working with clients/patients?

ranges.label: Low Burnout
ranges.description: Your overall burnout level is low. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Maintain your current work-life balance
Continue healthy coping strategies
Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance

ranges.label: Moderate Burnout
ranges.description: You are showing signs of moderate burnout. Mild stress is a normal part of life. Learning stress management techniques can help you handle it more effectively.
suggestions: Identify the main sources of burnout
Set boundaries between work and personal life
Identify your main sources of stress and try to address them gradually
Learn time management and prioritization skills
Set aside 15-30 minutes daily for relaxation activities

ranges.label: Severe Burnout
ranges.description: You are experiencing significant burnout across multiple domains. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional support
Consider making changes to your work situation
Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments

personal.label: Low
personal.description: Low personal burnout. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships

personal.label: Moderate
personal.description: Moderate personal burnout. Mild stress is a normal part of life. Learning stress management techniques can help you handle it more effectively.
suggestions: Identify your main sources of stress and try to address them gradually
Learn time management and prioritization skills
Set aside 15-30 minutes daily for relaxation activities
Maintain moderate exercise and healthy eating habits
Practice saying no and setting reasonable boundaries

personal.label: High
personal.description: Severe personal burnout. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities

Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships
